perturbation.original_text: 1. DEFINITIONS 1.1 Throughout this Agreement, and unless the context otherwise requires, the terms shown on Exhibit A (whether or not capitalized) shall have the meanings there specified. If other terms are defined in the text of this Agreement, then throughout this Agreement, those terms shall have the meanings respectively ascribed to them.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. DEFINITIONS 1.1 Throughout this Agreement, and unless the context otherwise requires, terms defined in the text of this Agreement shall have the meanings respectively ascribed to them.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the reference to Exhibit A in section 1.1, any terms defined in Exhibit A are now undefined within the main body of the agreement. Later sections, such as Section 3.3 [Proceeds], rely on definitions from Exhibit A (like 'Gross Proceeds' and 'Net Proceeds'). This creates an in-text contradiction because the agreement refers to these terms without a clear definition, leading to uncertainty in enforcement of financial obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.3 Proceeds: As a result of the different ownership interests, marketing relationships, and this Agreement, the proceeds of sales of AMR Product Suites shall be as follows unless otherwise mutually agreed in writing: 3.3.1 For sales of Water Meter AMR Product Suites to Datamatic, LKPL shall set the price of Water Meter AMR Product Suite sales to Datamatic. LKPL shall pay Source: LINK PLUS CORP, 8-K, 8/2/2005 Axiometric the higher of a) five-percent (5%) of the Gross Proceeds or b) twenty-five-percent (25%) of the Net Proceeds of all Water Meter AMR Product Suite sales.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the reference to Exhibit A, where crucial definitions like 'Gross Proceeds' and 'Net Proceeds' are located. Section 3.3 relies on these undefined terms, thus creating an in-text contradiction. Without the definitions from Exhibit A, the calculation of payments outlined in Section 3.3.1 becomes ambiguous and unenforceable, directly conflicting with the obligation to pay a percentage of proceeds.

perturbation.original_text: 3.  AUTOMATIC METER READING Axiometric and LKPL agree to jointly pursue accessing and commercially penetrating the AMR market by developing a suite of qualified and commercially marketable product suites for that market, marketing and selling that suite of products. The following shall be the general roles and responsibilities of the two companies with respect to AMR efforts: 3.1 AMR Products 3.1.1 Water Meter Development: Axiometric and LKPL are jointly developing an AMR product for remote reading of residential water utility meters (hereafter the Water Meter). The Water Meter is a hardware device with integral software. The software includes, under license, the Axiometric wireless mesh networking intellectual property. The Water Meter is capable of interfacing with a variety of water meter registers, recording water usage, logging various exceptional conditions, and reporting them via a radio link. The Water Meter is also capable of forming a mesh network with other Water Meters to extend the radio reporting range using the aforementioned Axiometric mesh networking intellectual property. The Water Meter software and hardware are based on existing Axiometric electric meter AMR hardware and software products; joint ownership of the Water Meter does not convey joint ownership of those existing products or of the mesh networking intellectual property. New software and hardware designs and intellectual property developed in the course of the Water Meter development are jointly owned; specifically, the algorithms used to collect data directly from a Water Meter using a point-to-point link (drive-by data collection), the specific hardware designs related to water register interface, battery power management, and other intellectual property specifically Source: LINK PLUS CORP, 8-K, 8/2/2005 described and mutually agreed in writing as amendments to this Agreement. Joint ownership conveys to each party individually the right to use, sell, modify, and create derived works from said intellectual property, as well as the right to sub-license these rights to others.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. AUTOMATIC METER READING Axiometric and LKPL agree to jointly pursue accessing and commercially penetrating the AMR market by developing a suite of qualified and commercially marketable product suites for that market, marketing and selling that suite of products. The following shall be the general roles and responsibilities of the two companies with respect to AMR efforts: 3.1 AMR Products 3.1.1 Water Meter Development: Axiometric and LKPL are jointly developing an AMR product for remote reading of residential water utility meters (hereafter the Water Meter). The Water Meter is a hardware device with integral software. The software is capable of interfacing with a variety of water meter registers, recording water usage, logging various exceptional conditions, and reporting them via a radio link. The Water Meter is also capable of forming a mesh network with other Water Meters to extend the radio reporting range. New software and hardware designs and intellectual property developed in the course of the Water Meter development are jointly owned; specifically, the algorithms used to collect data directly from a Water Meter using a point-to-point link (drive-by data collection), the specific hardware designs related to water register interface, battery power management, and other intellectual property specifically Source: LINK PLUS CORP, 8-K, 8/2/2005 described and mutually agreed in writing as amendments to this Agreement. Joint ownership conveys to each party individually the right to use, sell, modify, and create derived works from said intellectual property, as well as the right to sub-license these rights to others.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting "The software includes, under license, the Axiometric wireless mesh networking intellectual property. The Water Meter is also capable of forming a mesh network with other Water Meters to extend the radio reporting range using the aforementioned Axiometric mesh networking intellectual property. The Water Meter software and hardware are based on existing Axiometric electric meter AMR hardware and software products; joint ownership of the Water Meter does not convey joint ownership of those existing products or of the mesh networking intellectual property." from section 3.1.1, we removed Axiometrics explicit mention of proprietary wireless mesh networking technology that is part of the AMR water meter. Then in Section 3.3.5 'Payments to Axiometric for sales of the AMR Product Suite shall be in lieu of any licensed IP Royalty payments for those products' this clause now becomes contradicting since the explicit mentioning of the intellectual property was removed from the agreement, but the reference to 'licensed IP Royalty payments' implies such a license exists and must be accounted for.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.3.5 Payments to Axiometric for sales of the AMR Product Suite shall be in lieu of any licensed IP Royalty payments for those products.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that the AMR water meter includes Axiometric's proprietary wireless mesh networking intellectual property. The changed text omits this statement. This omission creates a contradiction with section 3.3.5, which refers to 'licensed IP Royalty payments' related to the AMR product suite. The omission of the explicit mention of the intellectual property in the modified text, while the contradicted text still refers to royalty payments suggests an IP agreement exits, making the terms contradict each other. This creates uncertainty and potential unenforceability, thus establishing a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. TERM This Agreement will remain in force for perpetuity or until and unless otherwise mutually agreed or amended in writing by both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. TERM This Agreement will remain in force for one year, automatically renewing for subsequent one-year periods, unless either party provides written notice of termination at least 30 days prior to the renewal date. If either party breaches the terms of this agreement, the other party may terminate the agreement immediately upon written notice.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement states that the term is for perpetuity, unless both parties mutually agree to amend it. The change now introduces a limited term of one year with automatic renewals, and adds a clause for immediate termination upon breach. The in-text contradiction arises in Section 7.2, which specifies a limitation on when actions can be brought (one year after the cause of action, or two years for nonpayment). If the agreement now has a defined term with potential for yearly termination, the clauses within 7.2 do not make sense, since the contract is only valid for 1 year, and there is a breach term that causes an in-text contradiction, of how long the contract would be in effect. This causes uncertainy if the cause of action occurs near the original end of the annual contract, or what the breach does to the limitations in 7.2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.2 All actions, cases, suits and proceedings in connection with this Agreement shall be brought in Maryland. All persons affected by this Agreement specifically consent to the personal jurisdiction of and venue in said courts. No action, case, suit or proceeding, regardless of form, arising out of or related to this Agreement, may be brought by either party more than one (1) year after the cause of action has arisen, or in the case of nonpayment, more than two (2) years from the date of the last payment. ALL ACTIONS, CASES, SUITS AND PROCEEDINGS SHALL BE HEARD WITHOUT A JURY. ALL PERSONS AFFECTED BY THIS AGREEMENT SPECIFICALLY WAIVE ALL RIGHT TO A TRIAL BY JURY AND SPECIFICALLY CONSENT TO THE PERSONAL JURISDICTION OF AND VENUE IN SAID COURTS.
perturbation.justification: The original text established the agreement's term as perpetual. The changed text limits the term to one year with automatic renewals and a breach term. This directly contradicts section 7.2, which sets limitation periods for actions related to the agreement (one or two years). The limited term and breach provisions in the changed text create uncertainty and conflict with the limitation periods in 7.2, as the contract's validity and potential termination now significantly affect when and if those limitation periods apply, causing a strong in-text contradiction.
